name: Cooperative generator-based coroutine scheduler
task: Create /tmp/eval_sched/sched.py with class Scheduler. Coroutines are Python generator functions; running coroutines yield values that the scheduler interprets: yield None means 'yield to next ready coro' (round-robin); yield ('sleep', t) means 'reschedule me after t seconds (monotonic clock time)'; yield ('spawn', coro) means 'start coro and continue without waiting'. Methods: spawn(coro) registers a generator and returns nothing, run() drives the loop until all spawned coros complete and returns. Implement with stdlib only — no asyncio, no trio, no curio.

validation.script:
import importlib.util, time, itertools; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_sched/sched.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); src = open('/tmp/eval_sched/sched.py').read(); assert 'import asyncio' not in src and 'import trio' not in src and 'import curio' not in src, 'must not use async frameworks'
# Round-robin yields
log = []
def coro(name, n):
    for i in range(n):
        log.append((name, i))
        yield None
sched = mod.Scheduler(); sched.spawn(coro('A', 3)); sched.spawn(coro('B', 3)); sched.run()
seq = [n for n, _ in log]
assert seq.count('A') == 3 and seq.count('B') == 3, f'log: {log}'
streaks = max(sum(1 for _ in g) for k,g in itertools.groupby(seq))
assert streaks <= 2, f'not round-robin: {seq}'
# Sleep test (out-of-order completion)
times = []
def sleeper(name, t):
    times.append((name, 'start', time.monotonic()))
    yield ('sleep', t)
    times.append((name, 'end', time.monotonic()))
sched2 = mod.Scheduler(); sched2.spawn(sleeper('X', 0.2)); sched2.spawn(sleeper('Y', 0.05))
start = time.monotonic(); sched2.run(); elapsed = time.monotonic() - start
assert 0.18 <= elapsed < 0.5, f'sleep timing off: elapsed={elapsed:.3f}s'
x_end = next(t for n,e,t in times if n=='X' and e=='end')
y_end = next(t for n,e,t in times if n=='Y' and e=='end')
assert y_end < x_end, 'Y(short sleep) should finish before X(long sleep)'
# Spawn test — child spawned mid-flight runs to completion
result = []
def child():
    yield ('sleep', 0.02)
    result.append('child_done')
def parent():
    yield ('spawn', child())
    yield ('sleep', 0.06)
    result.append('parent_done')
sched3 = mod.Scheduler(); sched3.spawn(parent()); sched3.run()
assert result == ['child_done', 'parent_done'], f'spawn ordering wrong: {result}'
print('OK')
